ai_summary: Validate meson masses and total decay widths from the Particle Data Group (PDG) Review of Particle Physics for specific light and heavy vector/pseudoscalar mesons.
general_notes: All values are from the Particle Data Group (PDG) 2024 Review of Particle Physics (https://pdg.lbl.gov/). Mass is the pole mass in MeV/c^2. Full width Gamma is the total decay width in MeV (related to lifetime by Gamma = hbar/tau). For narrow states, the width is very small (e.g. J/psi: ~0.093 MeV). These are the world-average values from the most recent PDG summary tables.

columns.name: Meson
columns.notes: PDG particle name with charge superscript (e.g. rho(770)0, phi(1020), J/psi(1S)). Use PDG naming convention.

columns.name: Mass (MeV/c2)
columns.notes: PDG world-average pole mass in MeV/c^2. Source: PDG 2024 summary tables. Express to 2-3 decimal places.

columns.name: Full Width Gamma (MeV)
columns.notes: PDG world-average total decay width Gamma in MeV. Source: PDG 2024 summary tables. For narrow resonances (J/psi, Upsilon) this is O(0.1 MeV) or smaller — report at least 4 significant figures.
